class.name: PinJoint2D
class.brief_description: A physics joint that attaches two 2D physics bodies at a single point, allowing them to freely rotate.
class.description: A physics joint that attaches two 2D physics bodies at a single point, allowing them to freely rotate. For example, a [RigidBody2D] can be attached to a [StaticBody2D] to create a pendulum or a seesaw.

members.member: If [code]true[/code], the pin maximum and minimum rotation, defined by [member angular_limit_lower] and [member angular_limit_upper] are applied.
The minimum rotation. Only active if [member angular_limit_enabled] is [code]true[/code].
The maximum rotation. Only active if [member angular_limit_enabled] is [code]true[/code].

If [code]true[/code], the relative rotation of the two pinned bodies is locked to the value it had when the joint was created, turning the pin into a rigid weld instead of a free hinge. The bodies keep their own collision shapes but a fixed relative orientation, so they move and topple as one rigid unit. Unlike [member angular_limit_enabled], which limits the [i]orbital[/i] angle of the bodies around the pin, this locks each body's own rotation.

Angular deadzone of the weld, in radians. Only used when [member angular_lock_enabled] is [code]true[/code]. The weld heals a relative-rotation error back toward alignment but stops this much short, leaving a tunable permanent free play (bend). [code]0[/code] heals fully back to the locked orientation.

How quickly the weld restores its locked orientation after being knocked out of alignment, when [member angular_lock_enabled] is [code]true[/code]. Follows the same sense as [member softness]: [code]0[/code] is a completely rigid weld that snaps back in a single step, while higher values make it spring back progressively more softly, approaching a plastic weld that holds whatever bend it was left in (the bodies still keep a fixed relative spin in all cases, so the weld never becomes a free hinge).
When activated, a motor turns the pin.
Target speed for the motor. In radians per second.
The higher this value, the more the bond to the pinned partner can flex.
